name: Ascent and return to Puig Campana (PR-CV 289)
name_original: Ascenso y vuelta al Puig Campana (PR-CV 289)
region_intro: Hiking around Finestrat offers diverse terrain, blending majestic mountainous landscapes with picturesque coastal scenery. The region is characterized by the iconic Puig Campana, a prominent mountain, and the Sierra Cortina, providing varied elevation and viewpoints. Trails often feature limestone cliffs, pine forests, and access to the Mediterranean coast, offering a mix of challenging ascents and more leisurely walks. Best hiking trails around Finestrat The most popular hiking route is Ascent and return to Puig Campana (PR-CV 289), a 9.8 miles (15.8 km) trail that takes 6 hours 10 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers significant elevation gain to the summit of Puig Campana. Another top favourite among local hikers is Puig Campana Summit – View of Benidorm loop from Finestrat, a difficult 7.6 miles (12.2 km) path. This trail provides panoramic views of Benidorm and the surrounding coastline from the mountain summit. Local hikers also love the View of Racó del Conill Cove – View of Villajoyosa loop from Castellum Romano, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through coastal areas, often completed in about 2 hours 35 minutes. Hiking around Finestrat is defined by prominent mountains like Puig Campana, coastal paths, and varied elevation changes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging summit ascents to moderate coastal walks. The routes in Finestrat are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 570 reviews. More than 3000 hikers have used komoot to explore Finestrat's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Finestrat?
A: Finestrat offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 50 routes available. These range from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents, catering to all fitness levels.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Finestrat?
A: Yes, Finestrat has 10 easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed walk. These trails often explore the lower slopes or coastal areas, providing gentle gradients and scenic views without significant elevation gain.

Q: What are the most challenging hikes in Finestrat?
A: For experienced hikers, the ascent of Puig Campana offers the most challenging routes. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e863023891/ascent-and-return-to-puig-campana-pr-cv-289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent and return to Puig Campana (PR-CV 289)&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31450723" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puig Campana Summit – View of Benidorm loop from Finestrat&lt;/a&gt; involve significant elevation gain to the 1,406-meter summit, rewarding with breathtaking panoramic views.

Q: Are there any circular walks in Finestrat?
A: Many of Finestrat's trails are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10329619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Racó del Conill Cove – View of Villajoyosa loop from Castellum Romano&lt;/a&gt; offers a coastal loop, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10356367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de les Marietes Trail – View of Benidorm loop from Finestrat&lt;/a&gt; provides a moderate inland circuit.

Q: What kind of natural features or viewpoints can I expect to see on Finestrat's trails?
A: Finestrat's trails offer a diverse range of natural features and stunning viewpoints. You can expect majestic mountain landscapes, such as the iconic Puig Campana, and coastal vistas overlooking the Mediterranean Sea. Many routes provide panoramic views of Benidorm and the coastline, often winding through pine forests and past limestone cliffs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4228929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font del Molí&lt;/a&gt; is a popular natural spring and starting point for many mountain hikes.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or attractions accessible via hiking trails?
A: Yes, Finestrat's trails can lead to interesting historical sites. The 16th-century Torre del Aguiló watchtower is accessible via coastal paths, offering historical context alongside stunning sea views. Additionally, the Tossal de la Cala Viewpoint features a Roman archaeological site and the Ermita de la Mare de Déu del Mar, providing a blend of history and scenic beauty.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Finestrat?
A: The hiking trails in Finestrat are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 570 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, which blends challenging mountain ascents with picturesque coastal scenery, and the rewarding panoramic views from peaks like Puig Campana.

Q: Are there any trails that combine mountain and sea views?
A: Absolutely. Finestrat's unique location allows for trails that seamlessly blend mountain and sea views. Hikes up Sierra Cortina provide unique perspectives of the interplay between the sea and mountains, with panoramic views of Benidorm and the coast. Even the challenging ascents of Puig Campana reward hikers with breathtaking Mediterranean vistas.

Q: Where can I find water or facilities along the trails?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4228929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Font del Molí&lt;/a&gt;, located at the foot of Puig Campana, is a well-known spring with fifteen pipes disgorging clear mountain water, making it a common and convenient stop for hikers to refill their water bottles before or after their adventures.

Q: Are there any hikes near Finestrat that feature lakes or reservoirs?
A: Yes, while Finestrat itself is known for its mountains and coast, the surrounding area offers trails near beautiful reservoirs. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/73676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadalest Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4041337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Amadorio Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; are notable highlights in the region, providing serene waterscapes and additional hiking opportunities.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Finestrat?
A: Finestrat enjoys a Mediterranean climate, making hiking pleasant for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable temperatures for hiking, avoiding the intense heat of summer. Winter can also be a good time for hiking, with cooler, crisp weather, especially for more strenuous mountain routes.
tips: It is the beginning of the path that takes you to the famous Vertical Kilometer. At the beginning of the route you will see the imposing cliff in the background. At the beginning, the path is covered by shadows from the trees and is in very good condition. As you advance, the vegetation will disappear and the terrain becomes scree and a super steep climb. Of course, the views will leave you breathless.

The Puig Campana, the Magic Mountain of Finestrat, is the second highest peak in the province of Alicante. A limestone mass that rises steeply to 1408 meters, a few kilometers from the Mediterranean and the tourist Benidorm.

The path reaches a fork where there is a marked cave, Cova del Cremat. You will see several paths that are not marked but if you follow them they will fade into the vegetation. The path that the route follows is the one that begins a descent with a steep slope.
